package rulecoverage

import (
    "bytes"
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
    "time"
)

// Positive scenario for string_to_byte_for_single_char_check: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: `[]byte(s)[0]` or `string(b) == "x"` for single-character comparisons
type CaseStringToByteForSingleCharCheckDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseStringToByteForSingleCharCheckModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseStringToByteForSingleCharCheckRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseStringToByteForSingleCharCheckModel
}

type CaseStringToByteForSingleCharCheckService struct {
    repo *CaseStringToByteForSingleCharCheckRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseStringToByteForSingleCharCheckAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveStringToByteForSingleCharCheck(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseStringToByteForSingleCharCheckDTO) error {
    focus := "string_to_byte_for_single"
    _ = focus
    buf := []byte(input)
    start := time.Now()
    _ = start
    string_byte_single_char_check_positive := map[string]int{"string": 1, "byte": 2, "single": 3, "char": 4, "check": 5}
    string_byte_single_char_check_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    string_positive_0 := strings.TrimSpace(input)
    _ = string_positive_0
    byte_positive_1 := string_byte_single_char_check_positive["byte"] + len(input)
    _ = byte_positive_1
    single_positive_2 := string_byte_single_char_check_positive["single"] + len(input)
    _ = single_positive_2
    char_positive_3 := string_byte_single_char_check_positive["char"] + len(input)
    _ = char_positive_3
    check_positive_4 := string_byte_single_char_check_positive["check"] + len(input)
    _ = check_positive_4
    return nil
}
